title: Wire checksum generation into the release workflow
action: Add a step (or a new job, using GitHub Actions `needs:`) to .github/workflows/release.yml that runs scripts/generate-checksums.sh (created in self-update-merge-config-1) against the packaged assets for every published platform, and publishes the resulting checksum(s) as release asset(s) via the same softprops/action-gh-release step already used for the other assets (release.yml:95-100). Note: EACH of the 3 Linux/macOS matrix legs (release.yml:22-34) is an isolated runner, same as the separately-defined Windows job (release.yml:102-179) -- a single merged checksums.txt across ALL of them requires artifact-passing (actions/upload-artifact from each producing job + actions/download-artifact in a merge job with `needs:` on all of them), not just for Windows. Prefer one merged checksums.txt (the approach.md-recommended convention) if the artifact-passing wiring is straightforward; if it proves impractical for this workflow's structure, publishing separate, clearly-named per-job-group checksum files is an acceptable fallback -- either way, every asset the release actually publishes must have a corresponding checksum entry attached to the release. Do not change any existing compile/package/upload step for the herdr-go binary assets themselves (per D8, D10 in docs/history/self-update-merge-config/CONTEXT.md).

truths: A git diff of release.yml shows only additive changes: every existing compile/package/upload step for the herdr-go binary assets is byte-identical to before
Every asset the release workflow currently publishes has a corresponding sha256 checksum entry published alongside it

The new checksum step/job did not exist in git history before this cell -- confirmed by `git diff` showing new lines referencing generate-checksums.sh, not merely present because of the pre-existing unrelated Get-FileHash step at release.yml:139-152 (third-party artifact check, not herdr-go's own assets)

artifacts.substantive: new checksum step/job wired to every asset-producing job via scripts/generate-checksums.sh, no herdr-go asset left without a checksum

key_links: release.yml's new checksum step invokes scripts/generate-checksums.sh for every herdr-go asset it covers, rather than reimplementing the hashing logic inline (Windows may use a native command only if scripts/generate-checksums.sh itself cannot run there)
prohibitions: No change to existing compile/package/upload steps for the herdr-go binary assets
Must not rely on the pre-existing Get-FileHash step (release.yml:139-152) to satisfy this cell -- that step checks a third-party downloaded artifact, unrelated to herdr-go's own release assets

trace.outcome: Wired scripts/generate-checksums.sh into release.yml: each build-matrix leg and the Windows job stage their packaged archive via actions/upload-artifact; a new checksums job (needs: [build, release-windows]) downloads all staged assets into one dir, runs the script to produce a single checksums.txt, and publishes it via softprops/action-gh-release. Purely additive (50 insertions, 0 deletions) per D8/D10 — no existing compile/package/upload step changed.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": [".github/workflows/release.yml", "scripts/generate-checksums.sh"],
  "tests_added_or_changed": ["No unit test surface for a GitHub Actions workflow; validated by YAML parse + content-anchor greps (the cell's verify) and git-diff additivity audit"],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Before-state: on the unmodified release.yml the cell verify fails because `grep -q 'generate-checksums\\.sh'` finds no match — the workflow published no checksum for herdr-go's own release assets (only a pre-existing, unrelated Get-FileHash step at release.yml:139-152 that checks a third-party downloaded Herdr preview binary). After the change the same command prints VERIFY_PASS.",
  "verification_run": "python3 yaml.safe_load(release.yml) parsed clean; grep generate-checksums.sh -> match; grep -i checksums -> match; git diff --stat -> 1 file changed, 50 insertions(+), 0 deletions(-), confirming every existing step byte-identical.",
  "deliberate_exceptions": ["Behavior only observable at release time in GitHub Actions (needs real runners + a tag push); not exercised live here. checksums.txt intentionally omits a self-entry, matching generate-checksums.sh's stdout-only, no-self-include contract and GoReleaser convention. Merged-single-file approach chosen over per-job fallback since artifact-passing wiring was straightforward (precedent: macos-install-smoke already uses needs: build)."]
}
trace.verify_output: YAML parses cleanly; both greps match (generate-checksums.sh and checksums present). Printed VERIFY_PASS. git diff --stat: 1 file changed, 50 insertions(+), 0 deletions(-) — purely additive, no existing step modified.
